subject: Product Management
title: Marc Andreessen's Definition
description: A quick, practical guide to Marc Andreessen's Definition.

hook.text: In 2007, Marc Andreessen wrote a blog post that gave product teams the single most important concept in startups.

buildup.text: He defined product-market fit as 'being in a good market with a product that can satisfy that market.'

discovery.text: Andreessen argued that market matters most. A great product in a bad market will fail; a decent product in a great market can win.

twist.text: He said you can always feel when PMF isn't happening — and you can always feel when it is. There's no ambiguity.

climax.text: When you have PMF, customers pull the product out of your hands. You can barely keep up with demand.

punchline.text: When it's working, you feel it. When it's not, you feel that too.

quiz.question: According to Andreessen, what matters most for product-market fit?
options: Being in a good market — market trumps product quality
Having the best technology in the industry
